title: Fluffy Venezuelan Rice Stew
description: A tangy, airy rice stew that captures Venezuelan flavors with a splash of lime, chili powder, and a dash of cumin, perfect for a solo dinner.

ingredients.name: Chili powder
ingredients.notes: Use a fine grind for smooth heat.

instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in tomato paste and continue cooking 2 minutes to allow it to caramelize slightly.
Sprinkle chili powder, cumin, and coriander; stir to coat the aromatics and cook for 1 minute.
Add uncooked rice to the pot, stirring to toast it lightly and coat with the spice mixture, about 3 minutes.
Pour in vegetable stock and lime juice, bring to a gentle simmer, then cover.
Reduce heat to low and let the rice cook for 15 minutes, checking occasionally that liquid isn’t evaporating too fast.
After 15 minutes, remove the lid, give the rice a gentle stir, then cover again and let it steam until the rice is fluffy and liquid absorbed, about 5-10 minutes.
Season with salt to taste, fold in chopped cilantro, and keep warm.
Serve hot, optionally with a squeeze of fresh lime or a side of avocado slices for extra creaminess.
